Name: PEBL
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — battery composition is a core advertised feature
How Batteries Are Defined: GUI Launcher lets the researcher select any subset of the 100+ test battery tests and chain them into a named "battery" run in sequence; battery configuration is saved as a file listing test paths
Task Ordering & Randomization: Fixed sequential order; no built-in randomization of task order within the battery (order set by researcher in config)
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No native multi-session scheduler; each battery run is a single session; sessions are tracked by data files written per test per session
Participant Tracking & Management: No participant-management portal; data files are saved locally per participant/session run, identified by a manually entered participant ID at launch

Name: Self-Regulation Ontology battery
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — the full study IS a multi-session battery (37 tasks + 22 surveys across ~9-10 days)
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined by the set of experiments checked into the Experiment Factory container + a study schedule config (participants complete subsets across sessions)
Task Ordering & Randomization: Fixed task assignment per session; task order within each session not randomized across participants in the original study design
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — explicitly multi-session; Experiment Factory's session token (unique URL per participant) persists state so participants can close the browser and return; cross-session scheduling managed at the study-administration level
Participant Tracking & Management: Sessions tracked by participant ID token; researcher monitors completion via flat-file output; no GUI management dashboard

Name: Experiment Factory (expfactory)
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — battery composition is its primary purpose
How Batteries Are Defined: CLI command specifies which experiments to include: `expfactory --run --experiments exp1,exp2,exp3`; generates a Docker container with those experiments in sequence; also supports `--generate` to produce a static folder
Task Ordering & Randomization: Task order set by researcher in the CLI/config; randomization of order not automatic but can be arranged at config level
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — a unique session URL is generated per participant that persists state across browser close; supports multi-session designs
Participant Tracking & Management: Participant sessions tracked by unique ID token; completion state stored in container flat files; no GUI dashboard — researcher inspects output files

Name: TestMyBrain / Many Brains Project
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — via TMB Custom Studies (contact-based, not a self-serve GUI)
How Batteries Are Defined: Researcher contacts Many Brains Project and selects from the full TMB Test List to define a custom battery; includes access to ultra-brief forms and alternate longitudinal forms
Task Ordering & Randomization: Order specified by researcher and communicated to participants (participants are directed to take batteries in a specified sequence)
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — explicit support for longitudinal alternate test forms and high-frequency assessment (ultra-brief forms); participants return to complete additional batteries over time
Participant Tracking & Management: Custom studies include bulk data download and automated data delivery; no self-serve participant portal — managed by Many Brains Project on the researcher's behalf

Name: Flowers Team Cognitive Test Battery
Supports Battery Composition: Fixed battery only — not configurable
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery is hardcoded: all 7 tasks run in sequence as defined in the Django app; no configuration of which tasks to include
Task Ordering & Randomization: Fixed sequential order (defined in source code); no randomization across participants
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No multi-session support — single-session design
Participant Tracking & Management: Participant progress tracked by Django session/consent records; no separate participant management panel

Name: cognitive-battery (thesimonho)
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — the launcher (`run_battery.py`) is explicitly a battery runner
How Batteries Are Defined: Researcher edits a config or the launcher script to select which Python task modules to include and in what order
Task Ordering & Randomization: Order specified in the launch script; no automatic randomization built in
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No multi-session support — local single-session run
Participant Tracking & Management: No participant tracking — data saved as local files identified by a manually entered participant ID

Name: Working Memory Test Battery (Tatool-based)
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — composed using Tatool's session/unit framework
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined as a Tatool study XML config specifying which task units (e.g., operation span, symmetry span) run in each session
Task Ordering & Randomization: Session schedule specifies order; some randomization possible within task blocks via Tatool unit settings
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — Tatool's session scheduler supports multi-session designs (sequential sessions across days)
Participant Tracking & Management: Researcher monitors participation via Tatool web interface; participant data stored in Tatool's database

Name: Tatool / Tatool-web
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — multi-task multi-session battery design is the framework's main purpose
How Batteries Are Defined: Study design defined as nested Units (tasks) and Modules (blocks of tasks) in a study XML config; can include multiple heterogeneous tasks in one study
Task Ordering & Randomization: Task/block order defined in config; adaptive training algorithms can alter what gets presented next based on performance; some randomization of stimuli within tasks
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — explicit multi-session longitudinal support with configurable session schedules (how many sessions, days between); home-based self-administration supported
Participant Tracking & Management: Researcher-facing web interface (Tatool Online) monitors individual participants' session completion and commitment in real time; bulk participant management

Name: ICAR
Supports Battery Composition: No battery management — ICAR is an item bank only
How Batteries Are Defined: N/A — researcher assembles ICAR items within whatever external delivery platform they use (Qualtrics, Concerto, etc.)
Task Ordering & Randomization: Depends entirely on the host platform
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Depends entirely on the host platform
Participant Tracking & Management: Depends entirely on the host platform

Name: Niv Lab jsPsych Demos
Supports Battery Composition: No built-in battery composition — each demo is a standalone jsPsych file
How Batteries Are Defined: N/A as distributed; tasks CAN be chained using jsPsych timeline code since each task is standard jsPsych, but no battery builder is provided
Task Ordering & Randomization: N/A
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No multi-session support built in
Participant Tracking & Management: No participant management

Name: jsPsych
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — the jsPsych Timeline IS the experiment/battery structure; this is the library's core design
How Batteries Are Defined: Batteries defined in JavaScript: a top-level timeline array holds nested timeline nodes (each node = a task block, with its own randomization, loop, and conditional settings); multiple tasks chained by simply appending them to the timeline
Task Ordering & Randomization: Full randomization support: `randomize_order: true` per timeline node; `loop_function` repeats a block conditionally; `conditional_function` skips blocks based on logic; unlimited nesting
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No built-in multi-session persistence in the core library; must be added via server/hosting layer (e.g., JATOS supports multi-session tracking)
Participant Tracking & Management: No built-in participant management; depends entirely on hosting layer (JATOS provides a study/participant GUI; Cognition.run provides link-based tracking)

Name: PsychoPy / PsychoJS
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — the Builder Flow panel composes a multi-task battery visually
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined in Builder as a Flow: ordered sequence of Routines (each Routine = one task or task block) connected in a visual flowchart; Loops nest around one or more Routines to repeat/vary them; blocks/counterbalancing supported via conditions files
Task Ordering & Randomization: Randomization via Loop settings (random order); counterbalancing via external conditions CSV; nested loops supported for block designs
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No built-in multi-session persistence; PsychoPy/PsychoJS produces a single-session experiment; multi-session requires external coordination
Participant Tracking & Management: No built-in participant management; Pavlovia provides basic session tracking/data management per experiment; researcher manually directs participants

Name: OpenSesame
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — tree-based structure of Sequences and Loops composes multi-block/multi-task batteries
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined as a hierarchical tree: Sequences chain items one after another; Loops repeat a Sequence with varied parameters; conditional `run if` rules allow branching; multiple task types composed by nesting in a top-level Sequence
Task Ordering & Randomization: Randomization: Loop item has a random order setting; counterbalancing via conditions table; `run if` rules enable conditional task inclusion
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No multi-session persistence in OpenSesame/OSWeb itself; single-session experiment; multi-session requires external coordination
Participant Tracking & Management: No participant management built in; hosting via JATOS adds session tracking/participant progress for OSWeb experiments

Name: Expyriment
Supports Battery Composition: Partial — code-level block/trial composition (no GUI)
How Batteries Are Defined: Batteries defined programmatically: Experiment → Block → Trial hierarchy; multiple blocks with different task types composed in Python code; no GUI battery builder
Task Ordering & Randomization: Randomization of blocks/trials done manually in code (e.g., `random.shuffle()`); no built-in counterbalancing helper
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No built-in multi-session support
Participant Tracking & Management: No participant management

Name: Psychtoolbox-3
Supports Battery Composition: No — no battery management tools
How Batteries Are Defined: N/A — sequencing is entirely manual MATLAB/Octave code; no battery framework
Task Ordering & Randomization: Manual code only
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No
Participant Tracking & Management: No

Name: PsyBuilder
Supports Battery Composition: No — GUI generates a single Psychtoolbox experiment
How Batteries Are Defined: N/A
Task Ordering & Randomization: N/A
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No
Participant Tracking & Management: No

Name: lab.js
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — Sequence and Loop components in the visual builder compose a multi-component study
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined visually: drag-and-drop Sequence components chain multiple screens/tasks; Loop components repeat a Sequence with varied parameters; components can be arbitrarily nested
Task Ordering & Randomization: Randomization: Loop has a random order option; sample-without-replacement option; `if` plugin for conditional branching
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No built-in multi-session persistence in the library; single-session study file; JATOS integration can add session tracking
Participant Tracking & Management: No built-in participant management; Open Lab hosting service provides basic study link/data management

Name: PsyToolkit
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — explicit battery composition by embedding experiments into a survey script
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined in PsyToolkit's scripting language: a survey script embeds one or more experiments inline using the `experiment` directive; experiments and questionnaires are mixed in a single study project; as many experiments as desired can be included
Task Ordering & Randomization: Task order is fixed by the script order; no built-in randomization of experiment order across participants (individual trial randomization within each task is supported per-task)
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No multi-session support — one study project = one session
Participant Tracking & Management: Participant progress tracked centrally on psytoolkit.org; data stored and downloadable from the platform's dashboard

Name: MindLogger
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — battery management is the platform's core architecture
How Batteries Are Defined: Batteries (called Applets) are composed in the no-code Applet Builder: researcher assembles multiple Activities (questionnaires/tasks) into one Applet, defines a schedule (daily/weekly/one-time), and assigns the Applet to a participant group
Task Ordering & Randomization: Activity order within an Applet is defined by the researcher; scheduled push notifications direct participants to complete activities at specified times; conditional logic can skip activities based on prior responses
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: Yes — explicit longitudinal/EMA design: recurring scheduled administration across days/weeks/months; supports both fixed-schedule and event-contingent triggers
Participant Tracking & Management: Researcher dashboard shows individual participant completion status; admin panel manages participant enrollment/groups/assignments; bulk CSV upload for scheduling

Name: Concerto Platform
Supports Battery Composition: Yes — flowchart-based test composition
How Batteries Are Defined: Battery defined as a Concerto "Test" flowchart: nodes represent pages/instruments/feedback blocks; arrows connect them in sequence; branching nodes route participants conditionally; multiple questionnaires or CAT instruments chained into one test protocol
Task Ordering & Randomization: Order defined by flowchart structure; conditional R-based branching routes participants to different instruments based on prior responses (adaptive branching)
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No built-in multi-session scheduler; Concerto tests are single-session by default; multi-session would require external scheduling and separate test links
Participant Tracking & Management: Admin interface lists all active test takers and their progress; access-controlled by user roles (administrator/test taker)

Name: IAT implementations (multiple independent repos)
Supports Battery Composition: No — single-task only
How Batteries Are Defined: N/A — each repo is a single IAT experiment
Task Ordering & Randomization: N/A
Multi-Session / Longitudinal Support: No
Participant Tracking & Management: No
